info.title: Precisely Public API
info.description:
# Documentation

Read more about our API in our help center [Precisely’s Contract Automation API](https://help.precisely.se/en/articles/4135833-precisely-s-contract-automation-api)

The Precisely API allows developers to create applications and modules that communicate with the Precisely platform.

## Authentication

Authentication can be done in two ways.

- bearerAuth, with a jwt from the /authenticate endpoint

- apiKeyAuth, with an api key in the X-API-KEY header [Helpcenter api-tokens](https://help.precisely.se/en/articles/5733009-api-tokens-in-precisely).

## Rate limiting

For API authenticated requests, you can make up 30 requests / minute per
organization.

The returned HTTP headers of any API request show your current rate limit
status:

| Header Name           |    Description                                                               |
|-----------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| X-Ratelimit-Limit     | The maximum number of requests you're permitted to make per minute.          |
| X-Ratelimit-Remaining | The number of requests remaining in the current rate limit window.           |
| X-Ratelimit-Reset     | The time at which the current rate limit window resets in UTC epoch seconds. |

## Templates, Projects and Documents

This is the structure of Precisely, everything starts with the template defining what a Project should contain.

### Template

The template consists of documentTemplates, references, approval rules and much more.

### DocumentTemplate

Defines the content of the document, default signees and default metadata.

### References

Defines questions to be asked when drafting a project, and the answers can be used in the content of documents as well as document metadata.

### Project

This is the result of using a template and answering the questions specified, a process we call drafting. It can contain one or more documents.

Approvals are applied on this level, and can be depending on answers given in the drafting process.

### Document

This is the actual document which can be sent for signing etc.

## Code examples

In our public repository with examples you can see how different usecases have been solved. If there is something missing let us know and we will try to expand the collection.

[public api examples](https://github.com/Preciselyco/public-api-examples)

## Resthooks

In order to avoid pulling the api for changes, there is now support for subscribing to events and receiving webhook callbacks when those events happen. This functionality in under development and a bit experimental, the related endpoints might change, before a final release.

### Security

In order to provide a way of verifying that the origin and payload is valid, the webhook callbacks carry a Precisely-Signature header.

```
"Precisely-Signature": [
        "t=1660034650,s=KdAi3tIpRM07fjjW6iAu6R97y5WYyGRXNBIHE41V4/c="
    ],
```

The signature is calculated as:

*t=the time of the event also included in the header. (unix time)*

*payload=the body content as json*

*endpoint secret=is returned when creating a subscription*


A SHA-256 hash, using the *endpoint secret* as key, of **t.payload\n** which then gets encoded with base64.

### Implementation examples

```python
# python example
import hashlib
import hmac
import base64

def computeRequestSignature(t, secret, payload):
    mac = hmac.new(bytes(secret, 'utf-8'), digestmod='sha256')
    mac.update(bytes(t, 'utf-8'))
    mac.update(bytes(".", 'utf-8'))
    mac.update(payload)
    mac.update(bytes("\n"))
    digest = mac.digest()
    return base64.b64encode(digest).decode()
```

```go
// go example
func computeRequestSignature(t uint64, payload []byte, secret string) string {
	mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))
	mac.Write([]byte(fmt.Sprintf("%d", t)))
	mac.Write([]byte("."))
	mac.Write(payload)
	mac.Write([]byte("\n"))
	return base64.StdEncoding.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))
}
```

```c#
// c# example
static string ComputeRequestSignature(string secret, string t, string payload)
{
    HMAC mac = new HMACSHA256();
    mac.Key = Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(secret);
    byte[] hash = mac.ComputeHash(Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes(t + "." + payload + "\n"));
    return Convert.ToBase64String(hash);
}

```

```
example information to test with

secret: 0cbb9eda-d89c-4b83-8faf-fd9f88217894
payload: {"id":"1699361960656-0","event":"events.document.status_changed","data":{"type":"document_event","object":{"id":"yd8sqKy","name":"combinedDocument","status":"draft","fromByName":"Tord Holmqvist","message":"","fromById":"gLdYiOg"},"link":"http://app.localho.st:80/archive/imported/862?organization=15","organizationId":15,"organizationName":"local"},"subscriptionId":"gb8bSEe"}
t: 1699361961 (2023-11-07T13:59:21+01:00)

Precisely-Signature:t=1699361961,s=+XK6b/ECp4AuQ63gBHF9tHzKq4HXdbAYrJ3UbtYci+k=

```

### Events payload

The events have id, event, data and subscription id properties. Different events can have different data.object payloads, while the type, link, organizationId and organizationName exist for all events.

```
# events.document.status_changed example
{
    "id": "1699361960656-0",
    "event": "events.document.status_changed",
    "data": {
        "type": "document_event",
        "object": {
            "id": "yd8sqKy",
            "name": "combinedDocument",
            "status": "draft",
            "fromByName": "Tord Holmqvist",
            "message": "",
            "fromById": "gLdYiOg"
        },
        "link": "http://app.localho.st:80/archive/imported/862?organization=15",
        "organizationId": 15,
        "organizationName": "local"
    },
    "subscriptionId": "gb8bSEe"
}

```

### Supported events

Which events that can be subscribed to can be listed with the [Events endpoint](#tag/Experimental/operation/getSubscriptionEventsUsingGET).

### Follow up / Troubleshooting

To see which events that were triggered for a specific subscription the [Callbacks endpoint](#tag/Experimental/operation/getSubscriptionCallbacksUsingGet) can be used. It lists the events from the last 30 days and if it was delivered successfully.

You can also resend an event by using the [Resend Callback endpoint](#tag/Experimental/operation/resendSubscriptionEventUsingPost).

#### Resends and disabling of failing subscriptions

Each attempt to deliver the callback will be retried 3 times.

Each subscription with failed deliveries will be retried every hour until there are 10 failed deliveries, in a row. Then the subscription is going to be disabled and must be activated again. A warning email will be sent to the creator of the subscription when this happens.

To enabled the subscription, use the [Update subscription endpoint](#tag/Experimental/operation/patchSubscriptionsUsingPatch)

get.description: Retrieves documents for an organization sorted by latest update

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: limit, or page size if total result set is larger than limit.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description:
page in pagination list. (Defined by headers) 
One way to do "safe" pagination is using sortBy=createdAt&order=asc which will put any new documents on the end of the result set. 
Otherwise updated/new items may skew the resultset during pagination.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: property by which the resultset is sorted.

parameters.name: order
parameters.description: dictates if the sorting should be ascending or descending.

parameters.name: onlyNewDocuments
parameters.description: onlyNewDocuments, which only is an alias for using createdAt as sortBy param.

parameters.name: includeContent
parameters.description: by default the document content is not included in the document list.

Pagination-Page-Count.description: Total page count in resultset, items devided by the page size.

Pagination-Page-Size.description: Current page size, as requested by the 'limit' query param.

Pagination-Page-Current.description: Currect page, as requested by the 'page' query param.

post.description: Returns a paginated set of documents for an organization given the provided query.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: limit, or page size if total result set is larger than limit.

parameters.name: page

page in pagination list. (Defined by headers)
One way to do "safe" pagination is using sortBy=createdAt&order=asc which will put any new documents on the end of the result set.
Otherwise updated/new items may skew the resultset during pagination.

Bad request: the national id provided does not meet the requirements of authentication method.

value.default_message: This party is already connected to the document

name.description: used to change the document name in the new project

isDraftIncomplete.description: Defines if the project should be considered incomplete (all answers have not yet been provided and the documents have not been generated) or not.

externalReferenceSource.description: An id or name of the source system of the entity referenced. It can be used to search for documents / projects related to the system in question.

externalReferenceId.description: An id in the source system that represents the entity referenced. It can be used to search for documents / projects related to the id in question.

x-codeSamples.source:
curl --request POST \
  --url https://api.precisely.se/organizations/{organizationId}/templates/{templateId}/projects \
  --header 'X-API-KEY: REPLACE_KEY_VALUE' \
  --header 'content-type: application/json' \
  --data '{"title":"My new project","documentNames":[{"id":1,"name":"My custom document name"}],"references":[{"name":"customer_name","value":"Kody Smith"}],"access":{"mode":"user","teams":["QyOJC4v"]},"isDraftIncomplete":true,"externalReferenceSource":"System A","externalReferenceId":"e5f15157-5f83-49be-852d-6d31538a7466"}'

post.description: Create project from template

name.description: used to change the document name in the redrafted project

publish.description: used to decide if new document versions should be published directly.

x-codeSamples.source:
curl --request PATCH \
  --url https://api.precisely.se/organizations/{organizationId}/templates/{templateId}/projects/{projectId} \
  --header 'X-API-KEY: REPLACE_KEY_VALUE' \
  --header 'content-type: application/json' \
  --data '{"title":"My redrafted project","documentNames":[{"id":1,"name":"My custom document name"}],"references":[{"name":"customer_name","value":"Kody Smith"}],"publish":true}'

tags.name: Experimental
tags.description: Experimental endpoints that might be subject to change without the usual deprecation notice

tags.name: Access
tags.description:
Access to an object can be managed by setting the access property. Three different levels are supported.
* USER meaning that the object is private to the user creating it and accessible by the user and administrators of the organization.
* TEAM meaning that the object is accessible by members of the specified teams and administrators of the organization.
* ORGANIZATION meaning that the object is accessible by members of the organization.
<SchemaDefinition schemaRef="#/components/schemas/Access" />

tags.name: MetadataChoice
tags.description: Metadata choice representing a valid choice for a metadata key. Every metadata key that is of choice-type defines a list of valid choices

tags.name: MetadataKey
tags.description:
Metadata key representing the different available metadata that can be set
on a document.

A metadata key has a value-type:

| value_type        | value                             | metadataChoiceId             |
|-------------------|-----------------------------------|------------------------------|
| "boolean"         | true/false                        | null                         |
| "date"            | "YYYY-MM-DD" (ISO-8601)           | null                         |
| "integer"         | <number> (only integers accepted) | null                         |
| "number"          | <number>                          | null                         |
| "string"          | <string>                          | null                         |
| "choice"          | null                              | <uuid> (from /metadata-keys) |
| "number+choice"   | <number>                          | <uuid> (from /metadata-keys) |

The metadata key also have a description and possibly an alias describing the metadata.

For examples look at the definition of MetadataPoint

<SchemaDefinition schemaRef="#/components/schemas/MetadataKey" />

tags.name: Project
tags.description:
the full representation of a Precisely project.

### isDraftIncomplete
The concept of incomplete projects is a way of starting the draft process without providing all the answers, and then the process can continue at a later time in the Precisely App or via the API.
Until the project is *complete* it doesn't have any documents and the operations that can be performed on it are limited.

### documents
The property represents the published versions of documents within the project.

  **CAVEATS**: All documents does not need to be based on the same version of answers.

### references
The property represents the questions asked when drafting the project. Some questions might be conditional which currently is not visible via the API, and therefore answers to those questions are not mandatory.

### answers
The property represents the lastest version of answers in the project. There can be multiple versions but only the latest is available through the API.

### formula values
The property represents the lastest version of values based on the latest version of answers.

<SchemaDefinition schemaRef="#/components/schemas/Project" />

bearerAuth.description: insert a token on the following format eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJleHAiOjE2MTk2Nzc3OTYsImlhdCI6MTYxOTA3Mjk5NiwidGdlbiI6MCwidXV1aWQiOiJlNjRhZjY0ZC05NDIzLTQ3ZWMtYjkxMC0xZWUxOGM4OTBjOTYifQ.CU8rC_XsifEbzzJnqgsusdejkvYvzqOO1875-sFJwbk

apiKeyAuth.name: X-API-KEY
apiKeyAuth.description: create a personal api token in the Precisely Application (see [Helpcenter api-tokens](https://help.precisely.se/en/articles/5733009-api-tokens-in-precisely) ) and use it with the api.

signingRole.description: Role of signee in the signing process, the different roles are provider specific and might not be supported by all providers.

authMethodToView.description: Authentication method the party must use to view the document, the methods are provider specific and might not be supported by all providers.

authMethodToSign.description: Authentication method the party must use to sign the document, the methods are provider specific and might not be supported by all providers.

mobile.description: Mobile phone number is required when using 'sms_pin' as authMethod, the number should follow the E164 standard and include the national prefix.

nationalId.description: The national id to be used with authMethod (no_bankid, se_bankid, dk_nemid, dk_mitid, fi_tupas, smartid)

signUrl.description: the external url to the signing provider, only avaliable if the signee have initialEmailNotificationDisabled = true

published.description: only one version can be published at a time

teams.description: Only used when mode 'team' is set.

content.description: contains the content of the published version

folderId.description: If the document is in a folder there will be a folderId. The root folder is represented with an omitted property.

SearchDocumentRequest.description: The query payload must contain either filters or metadata filters (or both).

v.description: the value to match against. (v or vs must be used, not both)

vs.description: the valueset to match agains. (v or vs must be used, not both)

metadataFilters.description: document metadata filters, the default operator is string_equals for metadata point values, but if the metadata is of another type the matching operator needs to be used.

k.description: the metadata key id, e.g. Contract value '2896461f-ccfb-45ff-b587-87c47fe8aa90',

SearchDocumentRequest.title: SearchDocumentRequest

email.description: nullable in combination with 'in_person' as authMethodToSign and hellosign as a provider

signingRole.description: Role of signee in the signing process, the different roles are provider specific and might not be supported by all providers.

authMethodToView.description:
Authentication method the party must use to view the document, the methods are provider specific and might not be supported by all providers. 
Some methods create additional requirements on the other properties - 'sms_pin' 
  mobile is mandatory

- 'no_bankid','se_bankid', 'dk_nemid', 'dk_mitid', 'fi_tupas', 'smartid'
  nationalId is mandatory and needs to meet the respective requirements for the national ids.

authMethodToSign.description:
Authentication method the party must use to sign the document, the methods are provider specific and might not be supported by all providers.
Some methods create additional requirements on the other properties - 'sms_pin' 
  mobile is mandatory

- 'in_person'
  email is not mandatory and only usable in combination with hellosign as a provider.

mobile.description: Mobile phone number is required when using 'sms_pin' as authMethod, the number should follow the E164 standard and include the national prefix.

nationalId.description: The national id to be used with authMethod (no_bankid, se_bankid, dk_nemid, dk_mitid, fi_tupas, smartid)

initialEmailNotificationDisabled.description: needs "Integrated Signing" enabled on the organization and only compatible with signing providers hellosign and scrive (at the moment)

finalEmailNotificationDisabled.description: needs "Integrated Signing" enabled on the organization and only compatible with signing providers hellosign and scrive (at the moment)

ListProject.description: A limited representation for listing projects

isDraftIncomplete.description: Defines if the project is incomplete (all answers have not yet been provided and the documents have not been generated) or not.

externalReferenceSource.description: can be used to describe a link to the source system.

externalReferenceId.description: can be used to describe an id in the source system.

clauses.description: if the type of the reference is "multiple-clause", there will be a set of clauses to select from. In order so select a clause its id should be set as the value of the reference.

options.description: if the type of the reference is "choice", there will be a set of options (which are the valid values)

conditional.description: the reference has a condition and is not mandatory if condition is not fulfilled.

defaultValue.description: the default, pre-filled, value for the reference if any.

isDraftIncomplete.description: Defines if the project is incomplete (all answers have not yet been provided and the documents have not been generated) or not.

externalReferenceSource.description: can be used to describe a link to the source system.

externalReferenceId.description: can be used to describe an id in the source system.

id.description: read-only attribute, not to be included in create requests

metadataChoiceId.description: UUID in the choiceGroup defined by the metadata-key. If the metadata-key would be for instance '6c656297-76db-4bf3-9c9d-14be2099fd15', which is language, a valid value would be 'e6fb5e1c-9073-44db-bd7d-f02bbebd9014' which is bengali

metadataKeyId.description: the example is "Vendor category", value type string

value.description: Depending on the metadata-key, value or/and metadataChoiceId should be set. The metadata key also provides the accepted values eg. (date/number/string)

MetadataPoint.title: MetadataPoint
MetadataPoint.description:
Metadata point represents a metadata value, which content depends on the metadata key and its value-type.

Examples:
Vendor category, value-type string
```
{
"metadataKeyId":"e28e4b9e-f554-41ee-a88e-c14cab183e0e"
"value":"system vendor"
}
```

Language, value-type choice, with a value representing Bengali (from the choice collection for language in /metadata-keys)
```
{
"metadataKeyId":"6c656297-76db-4bf3-9c9d-14be2099fd15"
"metadataChoiceId":"e6fb5e1c-9073-44db-bd7d-f02bbebd9014"
}
```

Start date, value-type date, needs a valid date as YYYY-MM-DD as input
```
{
"metadataKeyId":"43a06115-99f2-43f4-8595-49cb177f01c1"
"value":"2014-01-01"
}
```

Payment frequency, value-type choice+integer, needs a valid choice and a valid integer as value. So this example would represent a payment frequency of 'every 2 weeks'
```
{
"metadataKeyId":"43a06115-99f2-43f4-8595-49cb177f01c1"
"value": 2
"metadataChoiceId": "fdd3a95a-a0a3-47aa-b0af-89cec3da1fd4"
}
```

alias.description: descriptive name of the choice, can be empty if missing

names.description: alternative language map of property names

name.description: will return the english name by default, can be altered by using the Accept-Language header if the translation exists for the key, otherwise it will fallback to english.

alias.description: descriptive name of the key, can be empty if missing

choices.description: a list of valid choices that the metadata key can have

id.description: the id of the metadata key <uuid>

names.description: alternative language map of property names

valueType.description: defining valid values for the metadata key

name.description: will return the english name by default, can be altered by using the Accept-Language header if the translation exists for the key, otherwise it will fallback to english.

description.description: will return the english description by default, can be altered by using the Accept-Language header if the translation exists for the description, otherwise it will fallback to english.

MetadataKey.title: MetadataKey
MetadataKey.description:
Metadata key representing the different available metadata that can be set
on a document.

A metadata key has a value-type:

| value_type        | value                             | metadataChoiceId             |
|-------------------|-----------------------------------|------------------------------|
| "boolean"         | true/false                        | null                         |
| "date"            | "YYYY-MM-DD" (ISO-8601)           | null                         |
| "integer"         | <number> (only integers accepted) | null                         |
| "number"          | <number>                          | null                         |
| "string"          | <string>                          | null                         |
| "choice"          | null                              | <uuid> (from /metadata-keys) |
| "number+choice"   | <number>                          | <uuid> (from /metadata-keys) |

The metadata key also have a description and possibly an alias describing the metadata.

For examples look at the definition of MetadataPoint

message.description: contains the string representation of the message, no richtext information is preserved

sentAt.description: the timestamp of when the last invitation email was sent.

enableCollaboration.description: will collaboration features (comments and suggestions) be enabled for the reviewer.

attachmentKind.description: attach the document as the selected format in the email sent to the reviewer.

enablePdfAttachments.description: attach a PDF of the document to the reviewer in the email.

reviewURL.description: the invitation url for the new reviewer, that will be included in the email sent. Only available when creating a document reviewer.

objectId.description: the id of the document which the link points to.

objectRelation.description: the kind of relation for the link.

objectName.description: the name of the document which the link points to.

objectId.description: the id of the document which the link should point to.

objectRelation.description: the kind of relation for the link.

name.description: the name of the webhook subscription

callback.description: the url to which the callbacks should be done.

endpointSecret.description: on subscription creation a secret is returned to enable to verify the precisely signature header included in the callbacks.

insecureSkipVerify.description: show if subscription is configured to skip ssl validation of certificate.

name.description: the name of the webhook subscription

callback.description: the url to which the callbacks should be done.

enable.description: to enabled a disabled subscription, due to failing callbacks.

insecureSkipVerify.description: to disable ssl certificate validation for callbacks, e.g. to enable the use of self-signed certs.

callback.description: the url to which the callback was done.

callbackResponseCode.description: the http response code of the callback request.

eventPayload.description: the body of the callback request.
